question: [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate (line 672-674)] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate with seizedAssets=collateral[j] (exact full seizure) under preconditions where collateral[j] is exactly type(uint128).max cause UtilsLib.toUint128(seizedAssets) to succeed (seizedAssets fits in uint128), newCollateral = type(uint128).max - type(uint128).max = 0, and the clearBit condition to fire correctly, confirming no overflow in the bitmap clear path, but test whether seizedAssets=type(uint128).max+1 causes toUint128 to revert with CastOverflow before the clearBit is reached, leaving the bitmap bit set even though the intent was to seize all collateral? Proof idea: unit test with collateral[j]=type(uint128).max, seizedAssets=type(uint128).max, assert bitmap bit cleared; then seizedAssets=type(uint128).max+1, assert CastOverflow revert.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate (line 606-618, 670-674)] Can an unprivileged liquidator calling liquidate with collateralIndex=j under preconditions where the borrower has two collaterals at indices j and k (both in bitmap), and the liquidation seizes all of collateral[j] (newCollateral[j]=0, seizedAssets>0) clearing bit j, but leaves collateral[k] untouched with bit k still set, cause a subsequent liquidation call to correctly iterate only bit k in the bitmap loop, computing maxDebt from only collateral[k], and test whether the post-first-liquidation health check correctly reflects the reduced collateral set? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit (line 67-70)] Can an unprivileged actor trigger a scenario where clearBit is called with bit=collateralIndex where collateralIndex >= 128 under preconditions where the market has exactly 128 collaterals (MAX_COLLATERALS=128, indices 0-127) and collateralIndex=127 is the maximum valid index, confirming clearBit(bitmap, 127) correctly clears bit 127 via uint128(bitmap & ~(1<<127)), and test whether collateralIndex=128 would cause 1<<128 to overflow in Solidity 0.8 (revert) or silently wrap, violating the invariant that bitmap operations are safe for all valid collateral indices?
